_notes: Per-page schema — Workday is a multi-step wizard, not a single-page form.
Status: v1 scaffold. Only `my_information` page has selectors. Other pages
are TODOs surfaced so the wizard handler can detect them as 'not implemented'
and abort cleanly (instead of silently skipping).
Selectors below use Workday's data-automation-id attributes — stable across
tenants for the standard application wizard.

auth._note: loginAnchor → fail-fast needs_login. ONLY signInLink — 'accountLink' was REMOVED 2026-05-30: it's the SIGNED-IN account menu, present when authed, so it false-fired needs_login on every authed page (esp. my_information when Workday redirects straight there for an in-progress app). postLoginAnchor wraps EVERY in-wizard page: it both (a) vetoes the handler's login guard (inside the flow = never a wall) and (b) lets drain.isLoginWall tell an in-wizard nav teardown from a real SSO redirect (2026-05-30 DEVLOG). v1.5 watchMode SSO assist; v2 auto-click Google OAuth.

pages._note: Apply-method menu. MUST come before `posting` in this list: Workday's /apply page keeps the job-summary Apply button (adventureButton) in the DOM alongside the menu, so detectPage would otherwise re-match `posting` and re-click Apply forever (max_pages_exceeded). Pick Apply Manually — autofillWithResume triggers Workday's resume auto-parse which conflicts with fill-from-profile. After this: sign-in wall (→ needs_login) if not authed, else my_information.
Landing page is the job posting, not the form. Click Apply → opens the 'Start Your Application' method menu. No fields here. Listed AFTER start_application on purpose — see that page's note.

Selectors verified against real NVIDIA my_information DOM (2026-05-30); address selectors from the 2026-05-31 diag dump. Anchor = applyFlowMyInfoPage (specific to this page; applyFlowPage wraps EVERY wizard page → would false-match). Country + Country Phone Code are pre-filled by Workday (USA / +1) so untouched; Email is read-only. Address is now filled COMPLETE (addressLine1/city/addrState/postalCode), gated on profile.contact.address.street being set — fillPage skips an empty street, so we never submit a PARTIAL address (NVIDIA advances even with none, but a partial block can trip Workday's address validation). State is a button-listbox; stateFull maps the 2-letter profile value to Workday's full state name.

source._note: How Did You Hear About Us — required. value is a search seed; handler bestOptionMatches the live filtered options.

previousWorker._note: Have you previously worked for NVIDIA — default No (value=false).

phoneType._note: Phone Device Type — required. NVIDIA's options are Home / Home Cellular (no 'Mobile'); Home Cellular = personal cell. Confirmed from the live option list 2026-05-31.

phoneNumber._note: Strip the +1 country code — it lives in the separate prefilled Country Phone Code field.

addressLine1._note: Address Line 1 — fills only when profile.contact.address.street is set; fillPage skips an empty value. Filled together with city/addrState/postalCode so the address is COMPLETE, never partial.

addrState._note: State — Workday button-listbox (live automation-id countryRegion, diag 2026-05-31). Options are full state names → stateFull maps 'NJ'→'New Jersey'. Address Country is prefilled USA by Workday, left untouched.

pages._note: Real page wrapper = applyFlowMyExpPage (verified from live outerHTML 2026-05-31; the old myExperiencePage/resumePage guesses NEVER matched → wizard couldn't detect this page). Structurally unique (repeated Work Experience / Education panels, dual-spinbutton MM/YYYY dates, multiselect skills, file upload, URL fields) so it gets a dedicated code builder (`fillWorkdayExperience` in autofill.js) instead of the flat-field model — hence empty `fields` + `builder` flag. Required to advance: Work Experience 1 (Job Title, Company, From, To). Builder uploads resume first, fills WE panels from profile.workExperience, then auto-captures Education+Websites DOM to report.diag for the next build.

pages._todo: Tenant-custom dropdowns + free-text. Route fully through unknown-field scanner + AI fallback (same as Ashby/Lever path).
EEO / disability / veteran / pronouns — radios + selects. localResolveField + bestOptionMatch should handle most without AI.
Gender / race / ethnicity questions, US-formatted. Same resolver as voluntary_disclosures.

pages._note: Final page. No fields to fill — just click Submit. v1 wizard reaches this page only when intermediate pages are 'ready' (else aborts at the first 'todo' page). Submit selector is in the global FILL_SUBMIT chain too.

_v1_known_gaps: my_information + my_experience + review have ready=true selectors. The wizard aborts with reason='page_not_implemented' on the first remaining todo page (application_questions / voluntary_disclosures / self_identification).

Resume upload (my_experience) is v1 upload+Next. On the Apply-Manually path Workday does NOT auto-parse the PDF into the form, so a REQUIRED Work Experience / Education section would block Next (stuck_on:my_experience) — the diag then shows those fields to build. The file-upload-input-ref selector is unverified until the first live my_experience run.
Application Questions vary heavily per tenant — drain through unknown-field scanner, not adapter selectors.
Submit button click is the existing global FILL_SUBMIT path; the wizard's per-page next-button click is new code.
